files.README.md:
# Memory Demo

Small deterministic fixture repository for remem coding-agent A/B tests.
files.memory_demo/session.py:
def render_session_footer(title, budget_label):
    return f"{title}\n\u2514\u2500 Budget: {budget_label}"
files.memory_demo/slug.py:
import re


def normalize_slug(value):
    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", str(value).lower()).strip("-")
    return slug[:32]
files.memory_demo/visibility.py:
def visible_context_sections(sections):
    return [section for section in sections if section.get("status") != "suppressed"]

curated_context: # Curated Project Memory - Ticket keys normalize to uppercase PREFIX-digits. Accept spaces, underscores, hyphens, and leading #. If input has only digits, use default prefix MEM. - Retention policy names map to days: default or unknown = 30, audit_log = 365, ephemeral = 7, disabled = 0. - Slug normalization returns lowercase ASCII words joined by hyphen, trims boundary hyphens, limits output to 32 characters without a trailing hyphen, and returns `untitled` when the cleaned slug is empty. - Duration parsing bug root cause: values with suffix `ms`, `s`, or `m` must be converted to milliseconds; blank or None returns 0. - Current API endpoint is `/api/v2`; `/api/v1` is stale compatibility information and must not be restored. - Default storage driver is `sqlcipher`; old SQLite-only memory is stale. - SessionStart budget footer must render `Budget:

tasks.prompt: Fix ticket key normalization. The repository has only a weak visible test; use project memory/context for the durable ticket key convention.

history_episodes.summary: The team settled the ticket key normalization convention for benchmark fixtures.

memories.title: Ticket key memory convention
memories.text: Ticket keys normalize to uppercase PREFIX-digits. Inputs may contain spaces, underscores, hyphens, or a leading #. If the input contains only digits, use default prefix MEM.

tasks.prompt: Implement the retention window mapping. The visible test only proves the default; use project memory/context for the named policy windows.

history_episodes.summary: The retention policy windows were chosen after a governance review.

memories.title: Retention window memory policy
memories.text: Retention policy names map to days: default or unknown = 30, audit_log = 365, ephemeral = 7, disabled = 0.

tasks.prompt: Fix the slug normalizer edge cases. The hidden oracle checks the durable empty-output and truncation bug root cause from project memory/context.

hidden_files.tests/test_slug_hidden.py:
import unittest

from memory_demo.slug import normalize_slug


class SlugHiddenTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_empty_and_truncation_contract(self):
        self.assertEqual(normalize_slug(""), "untitled")
        self.assertEqual(normalize_slug("----"), "untitled")
        slug = normalize_slug("hello " * 20)
        self.assertLessEqual(len(slug), 32)
        self.assertFalse(slug.endswith("-"))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

history_episodes.summary: A prior bug showed empty slugs and trailing hyphen truncation were easy to regress.

memories.title: Slug normalizer root cause
memories.text: Slug normalization previously returned blank strings and sometimes left a trailing hyphen after truncation. Return `untitled` when the cleaned slug is empty, limit output to 32 characters, and strip any trailing hyphen after truncation.

tasks.prompt: Fix duration parsing. The old failure was caused by treating suffixed strings as raw integers; use memory/context for the unit conversion contract.

hidden_files.tests/test_duration_hidden.py:
import unittest

from memory_demo.duration import parse_duration_ms


class DurationHiddenTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_duration_units_convert_to_milliseconds(self):
        self.assertEqual(parse_duration_ms("250ms"), 250)
        self.assertEqual(parse_duration_ms("1.5s"), 1500)
        self.assertEqual(parse_duration_ms("2m"), 120000)
        self.assertEqual(parse_duration_ms(""), 0)
        self.assertEqual(parse_duration_ms(None), 0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

history_episodes.summary: The duration parser bug was traced to unit suffixes being interpreted as raw integer text.

memories.title: Duration parser root cause
memories.text: The duration parser must convert `ms`, `s`, and `m` suffixes to milliseconds. `ms` is already milliseconds, `s` multiplies by 1000, `m` multiplies by 60000, and blank or None input returns 0.

tasks.prompt: Fix the current API endpoint helper. There is stale v1 context in the code; use memory/context to choose the current endpoint.

history_episodes.summary: The v1 endpoint was invalidated by the v2 rollout decision.

memories.title: Current API endpoint
memories.text: The current API endpoint is `/api/v2`. `/api/v1` is stale compatibility information and must not be restored for new helpers.

tasks.prompt: Fix the default storage driver helper. The old SQLite-only decision is stale; use memory/context for the current default.

history_episodes.summary: The storage default moved from SQLite-only to SQLCipher.

memories.title: Current storage driver
memories.text: Default storage driver is `sqlcipher`; the older SQLite-only default is stale and should not be used by new helpers.

tasks.prompt: Fix SessionStart footer rendering for the budget label. Use project memory/context for the exact footer contract and the forbidden glyph behavior.

history_episodes.summary: The SessionStart footer contract explicitly forbids tree glyphs in compact budget lines.

memories.title: SessionStart budget footer contract
memories.text: SessionStart budget footer must render as `Budget: <label>` only when a nonblank budget label exists. Do not render tree glyphs such as `\u2514\u2500` in the budget line.

tasks.prompt: Fix Markdown table-cell rendering. Use memory/context for the escaping contract and avoid the explicitly forbidden HTML escaping path.

history_episodes.summary: Markdown table cells use Markdown-specific escaping and intentionally do not HTML-escape angle brackets.

memories.title: Markdown cell escaping constraint
memories.text:
Markdown table cells escape pipes with a backslash and render newlines as `
`. Do not use HTML escaping for this helper; angle brackets remain unchanged.

tasks.prompt: Fix workstream title canonicalization. Use memory/context to keep renamed current-memory-contracts follow-up work on one canonical stream.

history_episodes.summary: Current-memory-contracts follow-up titles should preserve one canonical workstream identity.

memories.title: Current memory contracts workstream alias
memories.text: Workstream titles for current-memory-contracts should canonicalize to `current-memory-contracts` across spaces, underscores, hyphens, and follow-up or cleanup suffixes.

tasks.prompt: Fix workstream status continuity. Use memory/context to prefer the latest active signal over an older paused row.

history_episodes.summary: Latest workstream status evidence supersedes older paused status rows.

memories.title: Workstream latest status continuity
memories.text: Workstream status continues from the latest nonblank signal. A latest `active` or `blocked` signal supersedes an older `paused` row; if no latest signal exists, keep the current status.

tasks.prompt: Fix feature flag pager routing. The answer requires combining feature ownership and team pager memory.

history_episodes.summary: Feature ownership was recorded separately from team pager routing.

tasks.prompt: Fix notification channel routing. Use memory/context to combine project identity and severity channel policy.

history_episodes.summary: Codex plugin notifications have project-specific release and incident channels.

memories.title: Codex plugin project identity
memories.text: Project `codex-plugin` is part of remem plugin operations and uses remem-specific notification channels.

memories.title: Codex plugin notification channels
memories.text: For project `codex-plugin`, release notifications go to `#remem-release` and incidents go to `#remem-incident`.

tasks.prompt: Fix due-date formatting. Use memory/context for the relevant user preference and ignore the ISO-style default in code.

history_episodes.summary: The user stated a preference for day/month/year dates in benchmark-facing summaries.

memories.title: User date format preference
memories.text: The user prefers due dates in day/month/year format, such as `25/06/2026`, not ISO year-month-day format.

tasks.prompt: Fix status-line formatting. Use memory/context for the relevant compact-output user preference.

history_episodes.summary: The user prefers compact status lines and does not want extra explanation appended.

memories.title: User compact status preference
memories.text: The user prefers compact status lines as `<subject>: <state>` and does not want extra detail appended in benchmark status summaries.

tasks.prompt: Fix current endpoint conflict handling. Use memory/context for how to choose among stale and active candidates.

history_episodes.summary: Endpoint candidates can conflict; only active non-stale candidates should be eligible.

memories.title: Endpoint conflict resolution
memories.text: When endpoint candidates conflict, select the active non-stale candidate with the newest `event_time`. Ignore stale candidates and inactive experimental candidates.

tasks.prompt: Fix owner resolution. Use memory/context for ambiguity handling: do not guess when multiple distinct active owners exist.

history_episodes.summary: Owner resolution should abstain instead of guessing when active owner evidence conflicts.

memories.title: Ambiguous owner abstention
memories.text: When owner candidates contain multiple distinct active owners, return None instead of guessing. Return the owner only when all nonblank candidates agree.
